steps.title: Identify the decisive contrast
steps.content: The passage states that neutron merger bursts typically last fewer than 2 seconds, then immediately notes that GRB 230307A lasted 200 seconds. That sharp contrast signals the blank must describe the burst’s duration as something unusual or unexpected.

steps.title: Eliminate wrong words and confirm the answer
steps.content:
Test each choice against the need for “something unusual”:
  • "a coincidence": means a point of correspondence or accidental similarity — the passage emphasizes difference, not similarity. ✗
  • "a reprieve": means temporary relief or a delay of punishment — a duration of time cannot be a reprieve. ✗
  • "an incident": means something that occurred — it fits the burst itself but not its duration, and carries no sense of strangeness. ✗
  • "an oddity": means something odd or unusual — a 200-second duration is dramatically unusual compared to the sub-2-second norm. ✓
That matches the correct answer — D is correct.
